description_long: This object is a vintage manual coffee grinder with a boxy, metal body and a hand-crank mechanism on top. The main body is made of dark, rusted metal with vertical ridges on the sides and a decorative, embossed front panel that reads 'F.X.B'. The top features a domed, fluted metal lid with a central spindle supporting a curved metal crank arm, which ends in a large, rounded wooden handle. The crank arm is slightly tarnished, showing signs of age and use, and the wooden handle is smooth and worn, indicating frequent handling. The base of the grinder is flat, allowing it to sit securely on a surface, and there is a small latch at the front, likely for accessing the ground coffee compartment. The overall appearance is utilitarian yet ornate, with a patina that suggests it has been well-used over many years.
description: A vintage, box-shaped manual coffee grinder with a rusted metal body, domed lid, and a curved crank arm ending in a large wooden handle. The front features an embossed panel and a small latch for accessing the interior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the embossed 'F.X.B' panel, domed lid, and the full crank arm with wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical ridges on the body and the rounded wooden handle of the crank.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, ridged back and the crank arm extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the crank handle positioned away, showing the boxy structure and ridged surface.
attribute_brand_logo: F.X.B embossed on the front panel
attribute_control_panel_type: none; manual crank operation with no visible control panel
attribute_chassis_design: boxy structure with decorative ridges and an embossed front panel
attribute_wheel_configuration: none; stationary base with no wheels
